---
title: "Lecture 13 Quiz: JavaScript Functions & Event Handling"
jupyter: julia-1.12
---

::: {.callout-note}
Review before quiz:
[Lectures](/lectures/lecture-13.qmd) | [Week 4 Content](/content/week-04.qmd) | [Lab 4](/labs/lab-04.qmd) | [Assignment 2](/assignment/02-assignment.qmd)
:::

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
using QuizQuestions
```

## Lecture 13 Self-Check

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. To style HTML", "B. To repeat reusable logic", "C. To create tables", "D. To store images"]
question = "Question 1: What is a primary purpose of JavaScript functions?"
answer = 2
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. `listenEvent()`", "B. `onEvent()`", "C. `addEventListener()`", "D. `attachHandler()`"]
question = "Question 2: Which method is commonly used to attach event handlers to DOM elements?"
answer = 3
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. It refreshes the page", "B. It stops default browser behavior", "C. It creates a new event", "D. It changes CSS automatically"]
question = "Question 3: What does `event.preventDefault()` do?"
answer = 2
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. `target`", "B. `type`", "C. `timestamp`", "D. `value`"]
question = "Question 4: Which event object property usually refers to the element that triggered the event?"
answer = 1
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```

```{julia cache=true}
#| echo: false
choices = ["A. `function add(a,b){ return a+b; }`", "B. `const add = a,b => a+b;`", "C. `add(a,b) => a+b;`", "D. `const add => (a,b)=a+b;`"]
question = "Question 5: Which option is valid JavaScript function syntax?"
answer = 1
radioq(choices, answer; label=question, keep_order=true)
```
